frame_analyses.description: Title card and program branding: 'MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills Programmes' from Barts Cancer Institute, Queen Mary University of London
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Institutional branding and educational context established

frame_analyses.description: FLS peg transfer trainer visible with 6 pegs arranged in circular pattern. Hands performing initial hand positioning on graspers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS equipment setup shown; trainer configuration matches official rubric specifications

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of laparoscopic grasper (appears to be Grasper brand) showing handle design and control mechanism
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment demonstration; illustrating proper grasper design for FLS task

frame_analyses.description: Full view of FLS trainer with 6 pegs visible, camera setup with monitor showing laparoscopic view, and cable management
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete trainer system demonstrating technology-enhanced learning environment

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic/laparoscopic camera view of foam appendage with blue markings and two graspers visible at base
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This appears to be Task 3 (Endoloop) equipment, not Task 1. Video content is mixed across multiple FLS tasks

frame_analyses.description: Charterhouse Square Campus sign identifying location at Queen Mary University of London
Academic speaker in office setting in front of presentation slide on 'MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills' with 'Technology-enhanced Learning' text visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Lecture/educational narration format with speaker explaining program

frame_analyses.description: Old Anatomy Building sign at Barts and The London, School of Medicine and Dentistry, showing Cancer Institute and administrative offices
Ligating loop applicator (Grasper brand) displayed on table showing handle design
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment for Task 3 (Endoloop); not Task 1 content

frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer setup with pegs visible and camera/monitor system for laparoscopic training
Speaker continues presentation at Barts Cancer Institute office with program slides visible in background

confidence_rationale: Video is clearly classified as instructional/educational material from the Barts Cancer Institute MSc programme. Multiple frames show lecture presentation, institutional signage, equipment close-ups, and narrated educational content. No actual trainee performance is recorded; therefore no task performance score can be computed.
cannot_determine: Actual task performance metrics (completion time, penalties)
video_type_summary: Educational promotional/instructional video for MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills programme at Barts Cancer Institute, Queen Mary University of London. Content includes programme branding, institutional context, equipment demonstrations, and expert lecturer introduction. Multiple FLS tasks are shown in equipment overview mode rather than as live performance recordings.

demonstrated_techniques: Laparoscopic camera positioning and monitor setup
Technology-enhanced learning environment for surgical skills
Institutional context of postgraduate surgical training
verbal_cues: Programme branding: 'MSc Laparoscopic Surgery and Surgical Skills'
Institutional affiliation: Barts Cancer Institute, Queen Mary University of London
educational_objectives: Introduce MSc programme structure and institutional setting
Demonstrate FLS equipment and trainer systems
Present educational philosophy of technology-enhanced learning
Establish credibility and expertise of training institution

recommendation: This video should NOT be scored as an FLS performance. It is instructional/promotional content designed to recruit and educate prospective MSc students about the programme at Barts. For actual FLS task scoring, a video showing a trainee or surgeon performing one of the five FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture) from start to completion is required.
